title: Roomio Lighting & Time-of-Day Contract
description: Owned by Agent E (Lighting). Describes the lighting state for a Roomio scene: per-room layered lights (ambient/task/accent), the directional sun, and the time-of-day + north controls. World units are METERS (matches src/three/coords.ts). Renderer is React-Three-Fiber (three ^0.169, fiber ^8, drei ^9): <Canvas shadows> => PCFSoftShadowMap; <Canvas flat> => NoToneMapping; legacy (non-physical) light units, so intensities are tuned to that renderer, NOT physically-based watts. This contract is ADDITIVE and forward-compatible: consumers must ignore unknown fields. A scene with no LightingState renders with E's built-in defaults (never a dark box).

timeOfDay.description: 0..1 across the day. 0 = dawn (sun on east horizon), 0.5 = noon (sun peak), 1 = dusk (sun on west horizon). Drives sun elevation + azimuth + warmth/intensity. Default 0.55 (pleasant early afternoon).

northOffsetDeg.description: North indicator rotation in degrees, added to the sun azimuth. Rotating swings where light comes from; +180 (or the 'reverse' control) flips it to the opposite side. Default 0.

barVisible.description: Whether the time-of-day bar UI is shown. Toggles INDEPENDENTLY of northVisible. Hiding it does not change the scene's current lighting (timeOfDay is retained).

northVisible.description: Whether the north/compass indicator UI is shown. Toggles independently of barVisible.

lightMode.description: Presentation / lighting-focus mode. When TRUE: all furniture is locked (no drag/rotate/resize) and the furniture editing hints are hidden, so the user can play with light without disturbing the layout. When FALSE (default): furniture is in its normal editable state. A single GLOBAL flag — furniture's own `locked` flags are never mutated, so toggling off returns every piece to its prior state. See contracts.AGENT_A_lightmode.

sun.description: The single directional 'sun'. One THREE.DirectionalLight aimed by position->target (NOT rotation); it is the PRIMARY (ideally only) shadow caster for performance.

enabled.description: When false, no sun/shadows; rooms still lit by their own lights + ambient fill.

maxElevationDeg.description: Peak sun elevation at noon (timeOfDay=0.5). Lower = longer shadows year-round feel.

warmthShift.description: When true, sun color warms (toward amber) and dims as elevation drops toward dawn/dusk; neutral-white near noon.

intensityScale.description: Multiplier on the time-derived sun intensity (renderer-tuned base ~1.35 at noon).

domeRadiusM.description: Distance (meters) of the sun position from house center along the dome. Must sit outside the house; shadow frustum is sized to the house, not this radius.

shadow.description: Sun shadow tuning. Scene-scale dependent (meters). Tuned values live in /shared/LEARNINGS.md. The orthographic frustum half-extent + far MUST enclose the whole house or shadows clip.

mapSize.description: Shadow map resolution (px). 2048 baseline; 4096 for large houses.

bias.description: Small negative value to fight shadow acne; too large -> peter-panning.

normalBias.description: Preferred for curved/angled surfaces; reduces acne without peter-panning.

halfExtentM.description: Orthographic frustum half-extent (meters) = max(houseHalfWidth, houseHalfDepth) + margin. Auto-derived from house bounds when omitted.

rooms.description: Map of roomId -> per-room lighting. roomId matches RoomDesign.id (single room) or House room_id (multi-room). A room with no entry gets E's default ceiling+ambient on first render.

lights.description: Layered light objects for this room. A default room ships with at least an ambient fill + a task ceiling light (P1). 'Layered' (passes A's rule) = >=1 task AND an ambient/hemisphere fill (>=2 layers).

id.description: Stable unique id within the room (e.g. 'ceil_1', 'amb_fill', 'accent_wash_1').

type.description: Light primitive / fixture kind. hemisphere|ambient = ambient layer; ceiling|pendant|desk|floor = task; sconce|wall_wash|strip|spot|point = accent (context-dependent — 'layer' field is authoritative).

layer.description: Which of the documented three layers this light belongs to. AUTHORITATIVE for A's layered-lighting rule.

color.description: Hex color the light emits (already includes warmth tint).

colorTempK.description: Optional color temperature in Kelvin (e.g. 2700 warm, 4000 neutral, 5000 cool). When present, 'color' should be the RGB derived from it.

warmth.description: Convenience preset (warm~2700K, neutral~4000K, cool~5000K). UI toggle.

intensity.description: Renderer-tuned intensity (NOT physical watts). See LEARNINGS.md for working ranges.

castShadow.description: Whether this room light casts shadows. Capped: keep few; the sun is the primary caster (performance).

isDefault.description: True for auto-added defaults (so the UI can label / reset them); user edits clear or keep this.

enabled.description: Soft on/off without deleting the light.

pos.description: World-meter position [x,y,z]. For ambient/hemisphere lights, position is ignored. Ceiling default sits near wallHeight.

target.description: World-meter aim point for directional/spot/wall_wash lights (optional).

fixtureModel.description: Optional visible fixture mesh to render for this light (swap a bare light for a pendant/floor-lamp/sconce). Render detail; does not affect the light contract.

contracts.AGENT_A_layered_rule: A's suggestion engine derives the 'light' role from FURNITURE with model==='lamp' (rules R1 'No light source', R-onlyone 'Only one light source'). Agent E proposes the engine ALSO treat a room as lit+layered when its LightingState satisfies E's predicate `roomLightingSatisfaction(roomId)` => { hasLight: bool (>=1 enabled non-shadow-only light), isLayered: bool (>=1 task AND an ambient fill) }. E exports this pure helper from /lighting/src/contract.ts. A's engine can import/mirror it so a room E lights passes R1 + the single-overhead rule out of the box. Pending A ack.
contracts.AGENT_A_lightmode: Light Mode (lightMode flag). A reads it from E's lighting store and: (1) in FurnitureEditor, treat a piece as non-interactive when `furnitureLocked(item, lightMode)` (= lightMode || item.locked) — guard the move/rotate/resize handlers and hide the rotate/resize knobs; (2) in Furnish, render the bottom editing hint only when `showEditingHints(lightMode)` (= !lightMode). Both pure helpers are exported from /lighting/src/contract.ts. The Light Mode toggle button itself lives in E's <LightingControls> overlay. Exact diff in /lighting/INTEGRATION.md.
contracts.AGENT_B_lightmode: Flythrough should honor the same presentation flag. Proposal: entering the flythrough (first-person walk / playback / record) implies lightMode=true (lock furniture + hide editing hints) for a clean recording, and restores it on exit; OR flythrough simply checks lightMode so editing and walking never fight over the same pointer. The shared 'presentation lock' = lightMode. No camera_path/scene_contract schema change.
contracts.AGENT_C_multiroom: Per-room lights keyed by House room_id; E reads rooms[].footprint {x,z,rotation,w,l} (cm) to place each room's ceiling light at the room center and to size the sun's shadow frustum to the whole house bounds. E needs window openings (kind:'window', sill, wallId, t) per room to aim accent/sun shafts; available via RoomDesign.openings (A) and C's worldWalls/openingsForRoom helpers.
contracts.AGENT_B_camera: Camera/flythrough records whatever lighting E sets; LightingState is part of the scene, no schema change to camera_path/scene_contract v1.0. If B wants deterministic lighting per recorded frame, read timeOfDay from LightingState (sun position is a pure function of timeOfDay + northOffsetDeg).
